findings.title: Conflation of newcomer ratio estimands
findings.description: The newcomer memo calculates a 4.49% IRS inflow vs 0.21% moved-from-abroad flow, implying a ratio of 21.38, yet reports a 'roughly 20.5x' ratio. This suggests the 20.5x figure is a median of county-level ratios, which is a different estimand than the ratio of medians.
findings.fix: Separate and name all newcomer ratio estimands: ratio-of-medians, median-of-ratios, and zero-denominator share.

findings.title: Ambiguous directional bias in newcomer comparison
findings.description: The claim that the domestic-vs-foreign gap is a 'lower-bound' is undermined by a timing mismatch between IRS (2022-2023) and ACS (2022 5-year). If foreign-origin flow accelerated late in the window, the ACS pooled estimate understates current flow, potentially making the gap look too large rather than too small.
findings.fix: Downgrade directional-bias claim to 'sign ambiguous' unless verified by a sensitivity grid across population thresholds and MOE filters.

findings.title: Invalid Wilson intervals on dependent splits
findings.description: The threshold-search null CI uses Wilson intervals over repeated random state splits. Because splits over a finite set of states are dependent, Wilson intervals (which assume i.i.d. Bernoulli trials) are not calibrated for inferential use.
findings.fix: Replace with descriptive intervals or use state-blocked/leave-one-state-out (LOSO) inference.

findings.title: Unjustified upgrade to moderate causal confidence
findings.description: The memo upgrades the stress-marker result to 'moderate causal confidence' based on geographic robustness, despite clean-window leads being non-null and employment pre-trends being negative. This jump from association to causality is not supported by the cited artifacts.
findings.fix: Implement a causal-confidence gate: forbid 'moderate' or 'high' ratings if clean pre-surge windows show sign instability or nontrivial lead effects.

findings.title: Underspecified newcomer ratio for zero denominators
findings.description: The median ratio for newcomer flow is reported without defining how counties with zero 'moved_from_abroad' shares are handled. For sparse data, this omission significantly changes the estimand.
findings.fix: Add an explicit machine-readable field for the share of zero or undefined ratio counties and document the handling of zero denominators.

findings.title: Improper source attribution for semantic claims
findings.description: Semantic claims regarding construct definitions (e.g., newcomer totals not being immigration-specific) are sourced to data parquets rather than the scripts or data dictionaries that define those constructs.
findings.fix: Cite scripts and data dictionaries for construct meaning; reserve parquet citations for data provenance.

findings.title: Lack of quantitative metrics for threshold surface diffuseness
findings.description: The claim that the threshold-location surface is 'diffuse' is qualitative and impressionistic. With only 100 splits across a 14-cell grid, selection shares move in 1-point increments without a formal concentration metric.
findings.fix: Quantify instability using concentration metrics like entropy, HHI, or actual-minus-null top-cell share.

findings.title: Non-falsifiable policy relevance claim
findings.description: The claim that 'policy relevance remains high' lacks an operationalized metric or success criterion, making it untestable and framing-sensitive.
findings.fix: Define a specific, falsifiable metric for policy relevance or tag the claim as [FRAMING-SENSITIVE].
